parsed_review.summary: CP00-824 (Platform Extensibility "Service Tail Permission Audit Bridge", Risk B, 40 units, RP27.P02.M03.S17-RP27.P02.M05.S12) is a descriptor-only pack and its claims are supported by the source inspected via Read/Grep/Glob. The manifest declares production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status:in_progress, implementation_layer:"descriptor", descriptor_only:true, writes_product_state:false, no_real_data:true, and production_ready_descriptor_verified:false, so there is no production/runtime overclaim. registry.js/validators.js build PlatformExtensibilityCp824ServiceTailPermissionAuditBridgeDescriptor (source_descriptor CP823) with a comprehensive no-write attestation; the CP824 validator enforces descriptor_only, runtime_execution:false, no API-key/webhook/workflow payload secrets, no real client data, no permission/audit/product-state writes, no runtime opening (api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed all false), Hermes gate H27 with emits_runtime_receipt:false, Claude gate C27 read_only:true promotes_final_approval:false, and handoff to CP00-825/RP27.P02.M05.S13. Coverage sums to 40 with per-deliverable counts matching the prompt distribution (failure_recovery 4, test 8, contract 2, implementation 16, security_audit 4, ui 5, claude_review 1) and SERVICE_ROWS slicing (M03 tail 6, M04 22, M05 head 12); the live closeout-pack-plan.json contains CP00-824 with deviation_from_plan:false and the same source_ledger_sha, so there is no plan drift, and the range is contiguous with closed CP00-823 (ends S16; CP824 begins S17). model.test.js asserts zero validation errors for CP824 binding, coverage, descriptor, and contract projection; models are tenant-scoped, matter-trace-required, and AuditEvent-referenced. The 522KB committed contract has latest_pack CP00-824, latest_projection descriptor CP824 (source CP823), historical_packs CP820-823, validation.valid:true, and an all-false closing no_write_attestation. Review hardening checks pass: auth preflight exit 0 with a 1436-byte well-formed JSON result (non-zero, not malformed, not fenced/prose, not tool-call-shaped), forbidden_tools_present:false, review schema present, summary_only review correctly disallowed (Risk B + security/audit + review-evidence) and not used, and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver, and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. Three non-blocking P3 boundary notes: (1) downstream closeout artifacts (command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present and, as a read-only reviewer without Bash, I cannot independently execute the 10 validation commands, so this PASS is conditioned on the deterministic closeout producing passing command-evidence and on human final approval before any runtime/enterprise-trust opening; (2) the production_ready_flag string label could be misread as an assertion while production_ready is false; (3) changed-file-scope.json classifies docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md as both an untracked implementation file and an unrelated preserved file. blocks_goal_closeout is false because the pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust; that authority boundary is acknowledged as external to this receipt.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts pending; 10 validation commands not independently executable by read-only reviewer
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts lists manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but docs/closeout-packs/cp00-824/ currently contains only manifest.json (the closed pack CP00-823 has all five). This is the expected pre-closeout state: this Claude review precedes adjudication/inspection and produces claude-review-result.json downstream; pack_level_claude_review.status is pending_review with empty valid_review_receipts. Because the review is read-only with no Bash, I could not independently execute the 10 expected validation commands (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check); I verified binding, validator logic, tests, live plan, and the contract projection by inspection only. The manifest still shows status:in_progress and production_ready:false/production_ready_descriptor_verified:false, so no claim is overstated at this point.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready and committing, ensure the deterministic closeout pipeline produces command-evidence.json capturing passing results for all 10 validation commands, plus adjudication.md and construction-inspection.json, and that human final approval is recorded prior to any runtime/enterprise-trust opening. Treat this PASS as conditioned on that command-evidence.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Expected pre-closeout state confirmed against the closed CP00-823 pattern; does not block pack or goal closeout. Surfaced to bound the scope of a read-only review.

findings.title: production_ready_flag label may read as an assertion while production_ready is false
findings.details: production_ready_flag is set to "platform_extensibility_service_tail_permission_audit_bridge_descriptor_verified" while production_ready:false and implementation_summary.production_ready_descriptor_verified:false. The adjacent _descriptor_verified:false and top-level production_ready:false make the true state unambiguous, and the *_flag string is a stable per-pack identifier (consistent with the CP820-CP824 bindings in registry.js and the contract latest_pack/historical_packs), not an assertion of readiness. A future reader skimming only the flag name could nonetheless misinterpret it as a verified claim.
findings.recommendation: Optionally clarify (naming or a comment) that production_ready_flag is a descriptor label, not a readiness assertion, and that production_ready/production_ready_descriptor_verified are authoritative. No code or data change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking readability nit. State is internally disambiguated by production_ready:false and production_ready_descriptor_verified:false.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists new-session-handoff.md as both untracked implementation file and unrelated preserved file
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in both untracked_implementation_files (line 144) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 151), which are semantically opposed categories. This is immaterial to the active pack boundary: the active scope is defined by active_pack_implementation_refs (the 5 modified RP27 files) plus active_pack_required_untracked_files (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-824), neither of which includes new-session-handoff.md, and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no required active file is dropped. The manifest worktree_transition_policy also treats new-session-handoff.md as unrelated/preserved. scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs and tools/progress-control-room/index.html are correctly kept out of the active refs and listed as unrelated dirty files.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the changed-file-scope generator so a preserved/unrelated file is not simultaneously emitted under untracked_implementation_files, to keep provenance auditing unambiguous. No impact on the pack deliverables.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking provenance bookkeeping note in the review tooling artifact (not a pack deliverable). Active pack scope remains correctly bounded.
